refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation period clarifies the requirement for design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime statistics over the evaluation period using telemetry data to verify availability meets or exceeds the target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability metrics to stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Use robust error detection and correction protocols on the link.
DP3: Deploy real-time link health monitoring and alerting system.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with failover control logic.
Failover mechanism depends on network link status inputs.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link redundancy and failover, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Risk of failover delay impacting availability if not optimized.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating error correction protocols (DP2) in next refinement level to further improve availability.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded systems responsible for network link hardware and failover control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for redundant network connections and failover signaling.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Reports link status and availability metrics to NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as network hardware and failover logic reside here; low diffusion risk.
Partial containment possible but higher diffusion risk due to software distribution.
Minimal containment; safety services monitor but do not control link availability.
System-level coordination but not direct control of link redundancy.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Vehicle module centralizes control of physical network redundancy and failover, minimizing interface complexity and diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential integration complexity with Teleoperation software components.
Hardware failover logic updates may require cross-team coordination.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review Teleoperation module involvement in failover signaling to reduce diffusion risk.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions and performance metric.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if failover logic is partially implemented in Teleoperation software.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for DP1, supporting clear ownership and manageable interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Telemetry interface enables effective verification of link availability from Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for link availability to Vehicle module.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface protocols between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for failover signaling.
Plan integration tests to verify failover performance and link availability metrics.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, refinement of interface definitions and inclusion of additional DPs (e.g., error correction) is needed to fully address availability and reduce diffusion risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability metrics to stakeholders.

revise.why: To incorporate complementary design parameters such as error correction and monitoring to enhance link availability robustness.

assumptions: Failover mechanisms can be implemented within Vehicle module.
open_risks: Integration complexity between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Potential delays in failover impacting availability.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating link availability over evaluation period.
Test results of failover performance under simulated link failures.
